Container.Name: isputnik
Container.Overview: Private self-hosted family media library for audiobooks, ebooks, photos, and other digital media. Includes a modern web interface, installable PWA, offline listening support, user management, and library scanning. On first run, open the web UI to create an administrator account.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Port used to access the iSputnik web interface.

Config.Name: Config & Data
Config.Description: Stores database, metadata, thumbnails, settings, and application data.

Config.Name: Media Storage
Config.Description: Optional media storage path. Additional storage locations can be added later by editing the container or from within iSputnik.

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Optional public URL (for example https://isputnik.example.com). Leave blank for automatic detection.

Config.Name: Cookie Secure
Config.Description: Use auto for automatic HTTPS detection. Use true only when forcing secure cookies.

Config.Name: Session Length (days)
Config.Description: Number of days a login session remains valid.

Config.Name: Invite Validity (days)
Config.Description: Number of days invitation links remain valid.

Config.Name: Trusted Proxy Hops
Config.Description: Number of reverse proxies in front of the app (usually 1 behind a reverse proxy or Cloudflare Tunnel). Leave blank for direct LAN access. Needed for correct per-IP rate limiting, auto-block, and trusted networks — set it to match your proxy count, no higher.
